Value: Effects of Selected Therapeutic Agents on Urolithiasis Induced by Terephthalic Acid in the Male Weanling Fischer 344 Rat
Negative control (vehicle) and Positive control (TPA only)
Food and tap water were provided ad libitum

Data Field: Loss to Follow-up (n per arm if reported)

Value: principally of TPA, calcium, phosphate and protein

Data Field: Baseline Stone Size or Burden (if reported)

Value: Male weanling Fischer 344 rat (CDF-COBS Fischer 344/CrlBr inbred albino rats)
4.0% terephthalic acid (TPA) in the diet for two weeks
0.200 mL/50 g rat vehicle (0.5% carboxymethyl cellulose in tap water)

Data Field: Outcome A: Stone Recurrence / New Stone Formation (DICHOTOMOUS)

Value: Diarrhea observed in positive controls and in pups exposed to TPA and treated with allopurinol or neutral phosphates
